headline: Orang Asli School Dropout Rate Remains at 42% Through Form 5
context: MOE data for 2025 shows 42% of Orang Asli students do not complete secondary education.

cards.big: 42% of Orang Asli students drop out before completing SPM examinations
cards.sub: Rate has barely improved from 45% in 2018 despite targeted programs

cards.big: Only 8% of Orang Asli students proceed to higher education as of 2025
cards.sub: National average for tertiary enrollment is 48% among school completers

cards.big: JAKOA manages 98 Orang Asli schools with curriculum identical to mainstream schools
cards.sub: No culturally responsive pedagogy or mother-tongue instruction is offered

cards.big: New Zealand Maori education reforms reduced Maori dropout from 35% to 18% since 2010
cards.sub: Community-led school governance was the most impactful intervention

cards.big: Forcing indigenous children into a monocultural school system is assimilation not education
Should Orang Asli communities have authority to co-design their school curricula
